segments.s481-g41: This is an adverbial + main verb + vocative sequence: 'به پاسخ' (lit. 'to/for an answer') functions adverbially as 'in reply'; 'چنین گفتم' = 'I said thus' with گفتم (1sg past) following the adverb چنين; and 'ای پادشا' is a vocative 'O king' directly addressing the addressee. Word order (adverbial — comment — verb — vocative) is typical of poetic/classical Persian inversion.
segments.s481-g42: که introduces a reported-content clause ('that' / 'who'). دانا modifies دل, producing the compound sense 'wise‑hearted' (a heart characterized by wisdom) or 'a wise heart'; مردم پارسا is a noun + adjective 'people who are pious' (the pious folk). The short clause therefore states a relation between a wise heart and pious people; the precise predicate linking is supplied by the surrounding lines.
segments.s481-g43: چو here is temporal/conditional 'when' (or 'if'); the following nouns are listed as small constituent parts — سوزن 'needle', پی is an older sense meaning 'tendon/flesh' (not the sense 'after' in the provided list), and استخوان 'bone'. بشمرد is 3rd person singular of شمر/شمردن with the b‑prefix common in classical Persian, giving a sense 'he/it counts' or 'he were to count' depending on the conditional frame set by چو.
segments.s481-g44: اگر 'if' starts the conditional; سنگ 'stone'; پیش 'before/in front of'; آیدش is آید + enclitic -ش 'comes to him/it' (3rd singular), so 'if a stone comes upon it/him'; بشکرد is an archaic/perhaps poetically inflected form meaning 'it breaks / it would be broken' (b‑prefix + root meaning 'break/shatter'). The sequence gives a counterfactual/conditional outcome.
segments.s481-g45: This opening phrase places the speech act: 'به پاسخ' ('in answer') followed by 'به دانا' ('to the wise one' / 'to the sage') indicating the addressee of the king's reply. چنین گفت شاه is inverted subject‑verb order typical of poetry: 'thus said the king' (گفت = 3sg past).
segments.s481-g46: که introduces the content of the king's speech ('that'). هر دل = 'every/any heart' (هر quantifier before noun). The second که is a relative 'which/that' introducing a subordinate clause modifying دل. آن گشته باشد combines آن 'that' + گشته (perfect participle 'become/turned') + باشد (subjunctive/present auxiliary) forming a periphrastic clause 'which has become that' or 'that has so turned', and سیاه is the predicate adjective 'black' used metaphorically (dark/evil).
segments.s481-g47: A triadic nominal/gerundive list introduced by repeated به marking manner/means: 'به بزم' (in feasting/banquets), 'به رزم' (in fighting/battle), and 'به خون ریختن' where خون ریختن is a verbal noun 'to spill blood' / 'blood‑shedding'. ریختن here is the infinitive/verbal noun 'to spill/shed', making the whole element a noun phrase denoting violent action.
segments.s481-g48: به هر جای = 'to/at every place' (هر quantifier + جای 'place'); با دشمن 'with/against the enemy' where با marks the adversary; آویختن is a classical verb meaning 'to cling to' or in martial contexts 'to fall upon/engage' (here: to attack/fasten upon the enemy). The whole phrase expresses repeated or widespread assault.
segments.s481-g17: بفرمود is an honorific/polite past verb ('he commanded/said'). تا introduces a purpose/result clause ('so that' / 'in order that'). گرد here is the noun object (literary/older usage meaning a lump/mass — listed sense 'dust' is the closest available), and بگداختند is the past plural of گداختن with the archaic prefix ب- (they melted). Together: 'he ordered so that they melted (it into a mass)'.
segments.s481-g18: از introduces the material ('from/out of the iron'), آهن is the material noun 'iron', یکی is an indefinite numeral 'one/a single', مهره‌ای is an indefinite noun 'a bead/amulet' and ساختند is the past plural 'they made'. Word order is the common Persian sequence 'from‑X one bead they made' with ساختند finishing the clause.
segments.s481-g19: سوی مرد دانا is a prepositional phrase 'to/toward the wise man' (سوی + noun + adjective). فرستاد is the simple past 'he sent'. زود is an adverb 'quickly/soon' placed after the verb for emphasis ('he sent ... quickly').
segments.s481-g20: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'. دانا is the subject 'the wise (man)'; نگه کرد is a common periphrastic verbal phrase (نگه 'look/gaze' + کرد 'did') meaning 'he looked/examined'. و links the clauses; آهن is the subject/topic 'the iron' and بسود is a predicate meaning 'proved profitable/useful' (here ‘به‌سود’ functions as 'was of use').
segments.s481-g21: به ساعت is an adverbial time phrase 'at that hour / at once'. ازان is 'from that' (referring back to the iron), آهن names the material, and تیره‌رنگ is a compound adjective 'dark-colored' qualifying the iron.
segments.s481-g22: یکی آینه ساخت is 'they made one (a single) mirror' (یکی = one/a certain, آینه = mirror, ساخت = made). روشن چو زنگ is a simile: روشن 'bright/ shining' + چو 'like/as' + زنگ 'bell' (here 'like a bell' i.e. shining/polished as a bell).
segments.s481-g23: ببردند is past plural 'they carried/brought'. نزد سکندر is a prepositional phrase 'to/into the presence of Alexander' (نزد = in the presence of). به شب is an adverbial time phrase 'by night / at night'.
segments.s481-g24: وزان = 'and/from that (thing)'; راز = 'the secret'. نگشاد here is the negated past form (ن‑ + گشاد) meaning 'did not open / was not disclosed' — in context it means the secret was not uttered. بر is the preposition 'on/upon/to', باد = 'wind', and لب = 'lip' — the idiom 'بر باد لب' = 'to the wind of the lip', i.e. 'to be carried away by the lips' meaning 'to escape by being spoken'.
segments.s481-g33: سکندر نگه کرد is a periphrastic past: the noun نگه (‘a gaze; a pause’) + کرد (‘did’) together mean ‘paused/looked/ceased briefly’. The coordinating و links that clause to او را بخواند; here خواند with او را (object marker) is a past tense verb meaning ‘called/summoned him’ (not ‘read’). Word order places the subject (سکندر) before the verb phrase, then the coordinated verb phrase.
segments.s481-g34: بپرسید is a past tense verb ‘he asked’ (classical/poetic past form). The conjunction و continues the sequence of actions. بر + زیرگاهش is a prepositional phrase meaning ‘on (his) low-place/bench’ and زیرگاه here denotes a low seat or place before the ruler. نشاند is an archaic past of نشاندن ‘to seat/set (someone down)’ — so the clause means ‘he made him sit (on the low bench)’.
segments.s481-g35: سخن گفتش literally ‘speech he-said-to-him’: سخن is the noun ‘speech/talk’, and گفتش is a contracted past-verb phrase meaning ‘he said to him’ (گفت + ـش = to him). از جام روغن نخست is a prepositional/topic phrase ‘about/of the cup of oil, at first’ — از marks the topic/concerned thing, جام روغن = ‘cup of oil’, and نخست = ‘first/at first’.
segments.s481-g36: همی is a classical continuous/temporal particle meaning ‘then/still’ (marks an immediately following action). دانش نامور is the noun phrase ‘renowned knowledge/learning’ (دانش = knowledge; نامور = famed). بازجست is a past verb ‘sought/sought out’ (he sought after it). Together: ‘then he sought renowned learning.’
segments.s481-g37: چنین is an adverb ‘thus/so’. گفت here is the verbal predicate ‘said’, with the indirect object phrase با شاه ‘to the king’ following it; مرد خرد (‘the man of wisdom’) appears after the verb in inverted, somewhat poetic word order (verb before subject), so the full sense is ‘thus said to the king the wise man’.
segments.s481-g38: که introduces reported speech (‘that’/‘that …’). روغن is the topic/subject ‘oil’; بر اندام‌ها is the prepositional phrase ‘upon the limbs/bodies’; بگذرد is a present/subjunctive-like form of گذشتن ‘to pass’ used here in the sense ‘be applied/come over’ (i.e. ‘that oil be applied to the limbs’). The clause is indirect speech reporting the advice about anointing the body.
segments.s481-g39: تو گفتی is the 2nd-person past ‘you said’. The conjunction که introduces the content of what was said. از فیلسوفان شهر is a prepositional/genitive phrase ‘from/among the philosophers of the city’ marking the class being compared or referred to.
segments.s481-g40: ز is the poetic form of از meaning ‘in/regarding’. دانش is the domain ‘knowledge’. مرا here is the classical dative/possessive construction (rendered in the wordlist as م + را) that indicates possession/experiencer ‘to me’ (i.e. ‘I have’), and خود is an intensive ‘myself’. فزونست is an archaic third-person copular/predicative form ‘is greater / is more’. بهر is a small adverbial qualifier (‘for/than/advantage’) — the whole phrase means ‘in knowledge I myself am greater/superior.’ Note the topical fronting (ز دانش) and the classical dative possessor construction (مرا) instead of modern Persian subject order.
segments.s481-g9: بیاسای is an imperative/hortative telling someone to be at rest. تا introduces a temporal/purpose clause 'until'. ماندگی is the noun 'the remainder' (object of the following verb). بفگنی is an archaic 2nd-person-form (classical b‑prefixed subjunctive/optative) from افگَندن/افکندن meaning 'you cast/throw away' — together the clause means 'be still until you cast away the remainder'.
segments.s481-g10: به here is a preposition 'by/with' marking cause ('by means of'). دانش is 'knowledge/skill'. م is an abbreviated/copulative element (classical/poetic) functioning like an implicit 'is' (the copula is omitted in prose but present conceptually). را is the object/definite marker attached to the following phrase. جان و مغز is the coordinated noun phrase 'life and marrow/brain' and آگنی is a post‑nominal adjective/participial adjective 'fiery, inflamed' meaning 'filled with fire' — overall: 'by knowledge my life and marrow are aflame.'
segments.s481-g11: چو is the subordinating conjunction 'when'. دانا is the subject 'the wise (man)'. به روغن is a prepositional phrase 'at/to the oil' (object of gaze). نگه کرد is the compound verb from نگاه کردن in the past tense 'looked' (نگه here is the noun/verb stem 'gaze' combined with the light verb کرد). گفت follows as the speech verb 'he said', introducing reported speech.
segments.s481-g12: که introduces the content clause 'that ...'. این بند is the nominative phrase 'this bond/knot/fetter'. بر من is a dative/benefactive phrase 'for me' or 'upon me'. نشاید is a classical impersonal/modal present meaning 'it is not fitting / it cannot be', and نهفت is a verbal adjective/past participle 'hidden/concealed' used with نشاید to mean 'cannot be concealed' or 'ought not to be hidden'.
segments.s481-g13: به جام اندر is a locative prepositional phrase 'into the cup' (اندر = 'in/into'). افگند is the past tense 'threw/cast' (from افکندن/افگاندن). سوزن هزار is the object 'a thousand needles' (classical order places the numeral after or next to the noun). The whole word order is verb with preposed locative: 'into the cup he cast a thousand needles.'
segments.s481-g14: فرستاد is the past tense 'he sent/dispatch(ed)'. بازش is باز + the enclitic third‑person object pronoun -ش, meaning 'them/back it' — here it refers back to the needles ('sent them back'). سوی شهریار is the directional phrase 'toward the king'.
segments.s481-g15: به سوزن is a prepositional phrase 'at/on the needle' marking the object of the look. نگه کرد is the compound 'fixed his gaze / looked' (نگه = gaze, کرد = light verb in past). شاه جهان 'the king of the world' is the subject; in poetry the subject often follows the verb, so the verb phrase appears before the subject here.
segments.s481-g16: بیاورد is a past-tense verb 'he brought / he had brought'. آهنگران is the direct object 'the blacksmiths/metalworkers' and is marked by را as definite object marker. نهان follows the object and functions adverbially/adjectivally 'in secret' or 'secretly/hiddenly' — so the phrase means 'had the smiths brought secretly'.
segments.s481-g1: چو here is the subordinating conjunction 'when' introducing a temporal clause. شد is the past tense auxiliary 'became/was' used with ساخته to indicate completion (literally 'became made'). کار آن سروبن ساخته is a noun phrase 'the work/affair of that cypress-trunk being made' (کار ... ساخته = 'the work was made' / 'the task was completed'). Word order is topic — verb: temporal marker + auxiliary + noun phrase describing what was finished.
segments.s481-g2: به آیین او جای پرداخته is a short result/statement clause: به آیین او 'according to his custom/manner' modifies جای پرداخته. جای is 'place/position' and پرداخته is a past participle/compound meaning 'placed/treated/arranged' (here passive/complete sense: 'has been placed'). The prepositional phrase (به آیین او) precedes the noun it qualifies, a common classical order.
segments.s481-g3: بپرداخت (بپرداخت) is a past-tense verb meaning 'he paid attention to / he dealt with' — here 'he turned to' or 'addressed'. ازان پس is a temporal phrase 'after that / thereupon' modifying the verb. به داننده مرد is the prepositional object 'to the learned/knowing man' (به + noun); داننده is a deverbal adjective 'one who knows'.
segments.s481-g4: که introduces a relative clause 'who/that'. چون here functions as 'when/as' introducing the subordinate temporal clause. خیزد is present/subjunctive form of خاستن 'to rise' (classical present-tense form used to describe a habitual or punctual action). از دانش means 'from/away from learning' and اندر نبرد 'into the battle/war' (اندر = in/into + نبرد = battle). The clause describes the man's relation to warfare when he 'rises' from learning.
segments.s481-g5: پرِ از روغن گاو جامی بزرگ is a noun phrase: پر از 'full of' (پر used as adjective), روغن گاو 'cow's oil' (genitive/attributive), جامی بزرگ 'a large cup/goblet' (جامی = 'a cup, goblet' used adjectivally). Classical Persian allows this stacked modifier order (quantity/content → container → size).
segments.s481-g6: فرستاد is the past-tense verb 'he sent'. زی here is a short prepositional element; the senses offered in the glossary don't match the exact classical function 'to/for' perfectly, so it's treated as a directional/relational particle leading to the recipient. فیلسوف is the noun 'philosopher'; سترگ follows as an epithet. Word order is verb → direction/recipient phrase.
segments.s481-g7: که introduces reported speech or an instruction 'that / saying that'. این را is the definite object 'this' with the object marker را. به اندام‌ها 'onto the limbs/bodies' marks the target. در بمال is an imperative: بمال (2nd person imperative 'rub!') with در giving the idea 'rub in / into'. The clause functions as a command about how to apply the oil.
segments.s481-g8: This is a list of target parts joined by و 'and'. The items are presented in classical order; each noun names a place to apply the ointment (head, middle, front, back, mane). The conjunction و simply links the items in a coordinate list.
segments.s481-g57: چگونه is the interrogative adverb 'how' placed at the start. به راه آید is a verbal phrase where به marks direction ('into/toward') and آید (present/presumptive) 'comes' appears at the end in classical (verb‑final) order. این تیرگی ('this darkness') is the noun phrase that the verb refers to; in poetic word order the noun follows the verb for emphasis.
segments.s481-g58: چه here is an exclamatory/how adverb introducing the question; پیچم is the first‑person verbal form ('I twist / shall I twist') with the enclitic -م. سخن را is the definite/direct object ('the speech') marked by را. بدین is expressed here as ب + دین in the tokenization and functions as the prepositional phrase 'into this', modifying the verb (i.e. 'twist my speech into this...'). خیرگی is the noun 'astonishment/stupefaction' that names the result.
segments.s481-g59: ترا here is the object 'to you' (written as ت + را in the tokenization) and combines the 2nd‑person suffix/element with the object marker. گفتم is the speaker's past/presentive 'I told/said' (classical narrative), and از دانش آسمان is a prepositional phrase 'from/about the knowledge of heaven' where از marks origin/concerning and آسمان modifies دانش ('knowledge of the heavens').
segments.s481-g60: زدایم is a first‑person present/subjunctive/optative form 'I remove/cleanse' (classical usage for a future‑directed or volitional action). دلت is دل + enclitic ت 'your heart' (the object from which something is removed). تا introduces a purpose/result clause 'so that', شوی is 2nd‑person singular subjunctive 'you become', and بی‌گمان is the predicative adverb 'without doubt / certain'.
segments.s481-g61: ازان پس is a fixed phrase 'after that / from then on' (ازان = 'from that', پس = 'after'). که introduces the subordinate clause ('when/that'), چون is a simile 'like/as', آب is 'water' and گردد (classical subjunctive/present) 'becomes/turns'. به رنگ is a prepositional phrase 'in (its) color / into the color', with به marking the manner/relationship.
segments.s481-g62: کجا کرد باید is an interrogative inversion where کجا 'where' is questioned and باید 'it is necessary / should' combines with کرد ('do/make') — classical word order places the modal with the verb around the question. بدو is the dative 'to him/unto it', and کار تنگ is a noun phrase 'a narrow/tight task' (adjective after noun); the whole clause asks where such a constraint should be applied to him.
segments.s481-g63: پسند آمدش is an impersonal/experiential expression 'it pleased him' (آمد used with پسند as the thing that 'comes' to someone), where the enclitic -ش marks the experiencer 'to him'. تازه گفتار اوی is the noun phrase 'his fresh speech/utterance' with تازه 'fresh' qualifying گفتار and اوی functioning as the possessive 'his'.
segments.s481-g64: دلش is 'his heart' (دل + enclitic -ش). تیزتر گشت is a comparative construction 'grew keener / became sharper' (تیز + تر comparative + گشت 'became'). بر کار اوی is a prepositional phrase 'toward/regarding his work' where بر indicates direction/attitude and اوی is the possessive 'his'.
segments.s481-g81: بفرمای is an optative/imperative request meaning 'grant' or 'let it be' (verb first, classical/polite form). تا introduces the subordinate/result clause ('so that' / 'that'), followed by the demonstrative این ('this') and برد ('victory') as the clause's theme. باز جای literally 'again place' = 'again a place/seat' — adjective/particle باز (again) precedes the noun جای (place/seat).
segments.s481-g82: خرد باد is an optative formula: noun + باد (here: 'may it be / may there be'), giving 'may wisdom be...'. جان + م (here written as separated م) = 'my soul' (م is the first-person possessive suffix attached to جان), and را is the classical object/emphatic marker attached after the possessed noun. رهنمای is a nominal predicate meaning 'guide' — together the phrase means 'may wisdom be the guide of my soul.' (The isolated 'م' in the segmentation is the possessive suffix and does not carry the provided '#هست' senses.)
segments.s481-g83: سکندر is the subject 'Alexander.' بدو ('to it/at it') indicates the thing he is astonished about. ماند is the past tense intransitive 'remained/was left' (here 'was left/was held'), and اندر شگفت = 'in wonder/astonishment' (اندر = in/within; شگفت = wonder). The clause means 'Alexander was left astonished at it.'
segments.s481-g84: ز is the poetic preposition 'from' or 'of.' هر گونه اندیشه‌ها = 'every kind of thoughts' (هر + گونه = 'each/any kind'; اندیشه‌ها is plural with the ezâfe/plural suffix implied). برگرفت is a past tense verb here meaning 'took away / removed / gave up' (he ceased or was deprived of such thoughts), so the clause reads 'he abandoned/was rid of every kind of thought.'
segments.s481-g85: بدو گفت = 'he said to him' (بدو = to him; گفت = 'said'—here past speech verb). زین پس is an idiom 'from now on' (زین is archaic demonstrative + پس 'after/then'). مرا is split in the segmentation into م + را — م is the first‑person object/possessive element ('me' / 'my' depending on construction) and را is the classical object/topic marker; بر گناه uses بر ('on/against/because of') with گناه 'sin' to indicate being held responsible for sin. The whole chunk sets up the reported plea/request that follows.
segments.s481-g86: نگیرد is a negative optative/jussive form meaning 'may (he) not take/seize/hold' (here: 'may [the Lord] not hold [me]'). It is third person and expresses a wish. خداوند خورشید و ماه is a title in apposition: 'the Lord (of) Sun and Moon' — خداوند (Lord/God), خورشید (sun), و (and), ماه (moon). The verb expresses the wish that this Lord not 'take' or 'charge' him with sin.
segments.s481-g87: خریدارم is a first-person form built on خریدار ('buyer'/'taker') with the suffix -م ('I') and functions idiomatically as 'I take/accept' (literally 'I am the buyer'). این رای و پند ترا = 'this counsel and your advice' (این = this, رای = counsel/opinion, و = and, پند = advice; ت (here the 2nd-person suffix) + را is the classical way to mark 'your'/'to you' or to attach the pronoun to the noun). را marks the whole phrase as definite/direct object of خریدارم.
segments.s481-g88: سخن گفتن is a gerund/infinitive phrase 'speaking / to speak' functioning as the subject of the following comment. سودمند is an adjective meaning 'beneficial/useful.' ترا again is the 2nd-person clitic + را marking the beneficiary/indirect object ('to you' / 'for you'); the clause reads 'speaking is beneficial to you.'
segments.s481-g73: This is a nominal list of required qualities. خرد (subject) is followed by باید, here functioning as a modal/copular-like predicate meaning “is necessary/one must have”; the following nouns (دانش و راستی) are joined by و and are included in that necessity. The sequence is typical of Persian: noun + باید to express necessity, with coordinate nouns after the first item.
segments.s481-g74: که introduces a following explanatory/relative clause (‘which/for they’). بکوبد is the classical-present form (b‑ prefix) of کوبیدن and here has the figurative sense ‘crush/strike down’; it is used as a general present (subjunctive/indicative flavor) describing what those qualities do. در کاستی is a prepositional phrase ‘in/where there is deficiency’ modifying the verb.
segments.s481-g75: مرا (classical dative/benefactive) is expressed in two pieces here (م + را) to mean ‘for me’ or ‘as for me’. خورد و پوشیدنی is a coordinated noun phrase ‘things to eat and things to wear’ (food and clothing). زین جهان = ز + این (archaic ‘from this’) + جهان ‘world’, i.e. ‘from this world’; the noun phrase serves as source/limit of what is sufficient.
segments.s481-g76: بس is the predicate meaning ‘is enough’ (copular ‘is’ omitted). از introduces the source ‘from the king’ (شهریار). آشکار و نهان are coordinated adjectives ‘open/public and hidden/private’, i.e. ‘whether openly or secretly’ — they qualify the source of the gift. The clause therefore states that such sustenance from the king, whether public or private, suffices.
segments.s481-g77: که again introduces an explanatory clause giving reasons. دانش is subject; به شب is a prepositional phrase ‘at night’ modifying پاسبان (‘guard’). پاسبان منست is an archaic copular construction meaning ‘is my guard’ (منست here a fused archaic/copular form expressing possession/predicate). The clause reads ‘knowledge, at night, is my guard.’
segments.s481-g78: This is another equational clause: خرد (wisdom) is the subject; تاج بیدار جان is a noun phrase ‘the crown of the awake (or waking) soul’ where تاج takes the following noun (جان) in a genitive/possessive relation and بیدار qualifies (awake/alert). منست is the same archaic copular/possessive form as above yielding ‘is my…’ or simply ‘is’. So: ‘wisdom is the crown of my wakeful soul.’
segments.s481-g79: به بیشی (‘for more’ / ‘for anything beyond this’) is a prepositional + comparative phrase expressing excess; چرا introduces a rhetorical question ‘why (should)’. شادمانی is the noun ‘rejoicing/joy’, and کنم is the 1st-person present-subjunctive/optative of کردن used idiomatically for ‘should I do’ or ‘I should…?’ — here used in the rhetorical question ‘why should I rejoice for more?’
segments.s481-g80: برین is orthographically condensed from بر این (‘on/for this’): بر (preposition) + این (demonstrative). خواسته is ‘request/wish’ (the thing to be guarded). پاسبانی کنم places the noun پاسبانی (‘guarding, keeping watch’) before the verb کنم; together they mean ‘I will serve as a guard / I will guard’. The verb ending (کنم) marks 1st person — ‘I will guard this wish.’
segments.s481-g65: بفرمود is a past/honorific verb meaning 'he ordered/said.' تا introduces a purpose/result clause ('that/so that') governing the following subjunctive. جامه و سیم و زر is a simple coordinated list (garment(s), silver, and gold) serving as the objects to be brought.
segments.s481-g66: بیاورد is the present-subjunctive/optative 3sg (‘bring’/‘be brought’) governed by تا. گنجور modifies جامی as an adjective (treasure-laden/treasury-related) and جامی is ‘goblet/cup’; گهر is a noun in apposition/attribute meaning 'gem/jewel' — together describing the kind of cup to be brought. The verb appears before its noun phrase, a common literary ordering.
segments.s481-g67: به introduces the indirect object/beneficiary ('to/for') and دانا is the adjective/noun 'the wise (one)'; سپردند is the past plural 'they entrusted/handed over.' و links the clauses; داننده is a noun 'the knower/expert' and گفت is the past 'said' (reported speech).
segments.s481-g68: که introduces reported content ('that'). من is the first-person pronoun 'I'; گوهری is 'a jewel' (object); دارم is present 'I have/possess'; اندر is an older/literary preposition 'in/within'; نهفت is an adjective/participle 'hidden, concealed.' Together they form the reported clause 'that I have a jewel hidden within.'
segments.s481-g69: که again introduces a subordinate/purpose clause. یابم is the subjunctive/optative form of 'to find/obtain' (here used as 'that I may obtain'); بدو means 'for him/to him'; چیز is a generic 'thing/thing of value' as the objective; و links the following predicate; بی دشمن است states 'is without an enemy' (بی + noun = 'without X' and است is the copula). Together: 'that I may obtain something for him, and (it/he) is without enemy.'
segments.s481-g70: نه negates what follows ('not'). چون here means 'like/as' (introducing a comparison). خواسته is used as 'desired'/'one's wish' (literally 'that which is wished'); جفت is 'mate/pair' (here 'mate'); آهرمن is the proper name Ahriman/the devil; است is the copula 'is.' The clause says 'not like one whose chosen mate is Ahriman.'
segments.s481-g71: به شب is a temporal phrase 'at night' (به used literarily to mark time). پاسبانان is the plural 'watchmen/guards.' نخواهند is the negative form of خواستن in third-person plural ('they will not/will not wish' — here idiomatically 'there will be no/they will not take'); مزد is 'wages/pay' and is the item negated/omitted. The sentence uses a terse, literary word order with verb before object.
segments.s481-g72: به راهی 'on/along the road' (راهی = 'road/way' or 'one who is on the road'); که باشم is a relative clause 'where I am' (که + باشم, present/relative 'that I be/am'); نترسم is first-person present negative 'I do not fear'; ز is the poetic preposition 'from'; دزد is 'thief(s)'. The clause means 'on the road where I am I do not fear thieves.'
segments.s481-g49: A noun phrase: سخن‌های (speech, with plural suffix -های) is modified by the adjective باریک which follows the noun in classical Persian to mean 'subtle/delicate words'. مرد خرد is a post‑nominal modifier meaning 'a man of wisdom' (مرد = man, خرد = wisdom). The whole phrase is the subject of the following clause.
segments.s481-g50: چو introduces a conditional ('if/when'); دل تیره باشد is the subordinate clause 'the heart is dark' where تیره follows دل as an adjective and باشد is the classical third‑person copula/subjunctive ('is/should be' here functioning as 'is'). کجا بگذرد is the main interrogative clause 'where will it pass?' with بگذرد as a present/subjunctive verb expressing possibility. The implied subject of بگذرد is the preceding noun phrase ('the subtle words').
segments.s481-g51: ترا is the preposed object 'to you' (ت + را split orthographically here) and گفتم is the first‑person past 'I said/I told'. این خوب‌گفتار خویش is the direct object: این = 'this', خوب‌گفتار = 'eloquent / well‑spoken saying', and خویش (خوی + ش) is a reflexive/possessive element indicating 'one's own' (here 'of mine'). Classical Persian freely places the object before the verb.
segments.s481-g52: A coordinated noun phrase: روان و دل و رای = 'spirit/soul and heart and counsel/opinion'. The adjective هشیار and the possessive خویش (خوی + ش) at the end modify the whole coordination: 'alert/sober counsel of his/one's own' (here, 'my alert counsel'). In Persian an adjective or possessive suffixed to the final member of a coordinate list commonly scopes over the whole list.
segments.s481-g53: سخن is the subject 'speech/words' and داند (دارد/داند in classical diction) means 'knows' or 'can' (i.e. 'is able'). از موی باریک‌تر expresses comparison/instrumentality: از + noun + comparative (باریک‌تر) = 'through/than a hair thinner' — an idiom meaning something can get through a very small gap. Word order places the instrument/comparative after the verb.
segments.s481-g54: ترا دل is 'your heart' (ترا here marking the person associated with دل; classical Persian often places pronominal markers before the noun). ز آهن is a poetic comparative 'than iron/from iron'. نه (the negator) precedes تاریک‌تر to make 'not darker' — the copula 'است' is omitted, as is common in poetry, so the predicate is understood: 'your heart is not darker than iron' (i.e. 'at least as dark as iron').
segments.s481-g55: تو گفتی is the past tense 'you said'. برین سالیان is an archaic time phrase meaning 'in/over those years' (برین = بر + این, an older form equivalent to 'بر این' = 'in those'), and برگذشت is the past 'passed by' (the verb governing the time phrase). The clause reports what the addressee had said about the passing of years.
segments.s481-g56: ز خون‌ها = 'from the bloods/bloodshed' (ز marks cause/origin). دلم is 'my heart' (دل + possessive suffix ـم). پر ز زنگار گشت is a verbal phrase 'became full of rust' where پر = 'full (of)' and گشت = 'became', so literally 'my heart became full of rust' — a metaphorical rust caused by (the) bloodshed.
segments.s481-g25: سکندر is the subject 'Alexander'. نهاد is the past tense of نهادن (to put/place) used here as a simple past verb. آینه is the direct object 'the mirror' and زیر نم is a locative phrase ('under/beneath the moisture/dew') following the object—classical Persian often places such locatives after the object.
segments.s481-g26: همی داشت is a classical progressive construction: همی + داشت gives an ongoing/past continuous sense ('kept/was keeping'). تا introduces a result clause 'until' and شد is the past of شدن 'became'. سیاه و دژم are predicate adjectives describing the result. In Ferdowsi همی is a frequent archaic continuous marker.
segments.s481-g27: بر فیلسوفش is a prepositional phrase 'to his philosopher' (بر + object with possessive -ش). Framed before the verb, it marks the recipient. فرستاد is the simple past 'sent'. باز here is an adverb ('again' or 'back') placed after the verb; in context it signals a sending back or a repeated sending.
segments.s481-g28: بران is a contraction of بر آن 'thereupon/for that' linking to the previous action. کار شد is a passive/impersonal phrase 'the work/affair was done'. رمز آهن دراز is a noun phrase: رمز 'token/sign' followed by qualifiers 'آهن دراز' = 'long iron (one)', so 'a long iron token/rod'—word order has the head رمز followed by its descriptive phrase.
segments.s481-g29: خردمند is the subject 'the wise man'. بزدود is the past of زدودن 'to remove/strip/clean off' used here as a transitive past verb. آهن is the object 'the iron'. چو آب is a simile 'like/as water' modifying the manner of the action (i.e., he treated or rendered the iron as if it were water).
segments.s481-g30: فرستاد is again the past 'sent'. بازش combines باز ('back/again') with the clitic -ش (object pronoun 'it/him'), so 'sent it back'. هم here is an additive/emphatic 'also/indeed' and اندر شتاب is an adverbial phrase 'in haste'—together: 'sent it back as well, in haste' or more naturally 'sent it back at once, in haste'.
segments.s481-g31: This is a sequence of causal/relational prepositional phrases: ز دودش 'from/ because of its smoke', ز دارو کزان 'from the remedy/medicine from which (it came)' — کزان is a relative 'from which' referring back to دارو — and پس ز نم 'after that/from the moisture'. Such stacked ز-phrases give reasons and circumstances before the main verb.
segments.s481-g32: نگردد is a negative present/future form (ن + گردد) meaning 'will not become' / 'does not turn into' in this context. به زودی is an adverbial phrase 'soon/quickly' modifying the verb. سیاه و دژم are the resulting predicate adjectives 'black and sullen'. Word order places the negated verb before the adverbial and adjectives as expected in classical Persian.

sentences.s481-s21: In reply I said thus, O king: that the wise heart belongs to the pious people.
sentences.s481-s22: When (one) counts needle, tendon (flesh) and bone, if a stone comes before it, it will be shattered.
sentences.s481-s23: In reply to the sage the king thus spoke: that every heart which has become that is black.
sentences.s481-s24: In feasting and in battle and in shedding blood, in attacking the enemy at every place.
sentences.s481-s9: He commanded that they melt it down; from the iron they made a single bead.
sentences.s481-s10: He quickly sent it to the wise man; when the sage examined it, the iron proved useful.
sentences.s481-s11: At once, from that dark-colored iron they made a mirror, bright as a bell.
sentences.s481-s12: They brought it to Alexander at night, and the secret did not escape his lips to the wind.
sentences.s481-s17: Alexander paused, summoned him, questioned him, and seated him on the lower bench.
sentences.s481-s18: He first spoke to him about the cup of oil; then he inquired after renowned learning.
sentences.s481-s19: Thus the wise man said to the king that oil should be applied to the limbs.
sentences.s481-s20: You said that, among the city's philosophers, in knowledge I myself am superior.
sentences.s481-s5: Rest until you cast away the remainder; by knowledge my life and marrow are aflame.
sentences.s481-s6: When the wise man looked at the oil, he said,
sentences.s481-s7: He cast a thousand needles into the cup and sent them back toward the king.
sentences.s481-s8: The sovereign fixed his gaze on the needle and had the smiths brought in secret.
sentences.s481-s1: When the work on that cypress-trunk was finished, it was set in place according to his custom.
sentences.s481-s2: Thereupon he attended to that learned man, who, when he rose from learning, did not take part in battle.
sentences.s481-s3: He sent a large goblet full of cow's oil to the philosopher.
sentences.s481-s4: Saying: rub this into the limbs — on the head, the middle, the front, the back, and the mane.
sentences.s481-s29: How has this darkness come upon the path? How shall I twist my speech into this astonishment?
sentences.s481-s30: I told you from the knowledge of heaven; I will cleanse your heart so that you become certain.
sentences.s481-s31: After that, when it turns like water in color, where then should one impose upon him a narrow (constrained) task?
sentences.s481-s32: The fresh speech pleased him; his heart grew keener toward his work.
sentences.s481-s41: Grant (let it be) that this victory again be a seat of wisdom; may wisdom be the guide of my soul.
sentences.s481-s42: Alexander was left astonished at it; he abandoned every kind of thought.
sentences.s481-s43: He said to him: 'From now on may the Lord of Sun and Moon not hold me guilty of sin.'
sentences.s481-s44: I accept this counsel and your advice; speaking is beneficial to you.
sentences.s481-s37: Wisdom, knowledge, and uprightness are necessary, for they strike down crookedness where there is deficiency.
sentences.s481-s38: Food and clothing from this world are enough for me, whether given by the king openly or secretly.
sentences.s481-s39: For knowledge is my night-guard, and wisdom is the crown of my waking soul.
sentences.s481-s40: Why should I rejoice for more? I will guard this request.
sentences.s481-s33: He ordered that robes, silver, and gold be brought, and a jeweled, treasure-bearing goblet.
sentences.s481-s34: They entrusted it to the wise man, and the knower said that I have a jewel hidden within.
sentences.s481-s35: that I might obtain something for him and be without an enemy; not like one whose desired mate is Ahriman.
sentences.s481-s36: At night the watchmen will have no pay; on the road where I am I do not fear thieves.
sentences.s481-s25: Where can the subtle words of a man of wisdom pass if the heart is dark?
sentences.s481-s26: I told you this eloquent saying of mine — my soul, heart, and alert counsel.
sentences.s481-s27: Words can slip through a hair thinner than this; your heart is no less dark than iron.
sentences.s481-s28: You said that those years passed by; my heart became filled with rust from the bloodshed.
sentences.s481-s13: Alexander placed the mirror beneath the moisture and kept it there until it turned black and sullen.
sentences.s481-s14: He sent (someone) to his philosopher; thereupon the task was done and a long iron rod (token) was made.
sentences.s481-s15: The wise man removed the iron as if it were water and sent it back at once in haste.
sentences.s481-s16: Because of its smoke and the remedy from which (it came), it will not soon, from the moisture, become black and sullen.
